creator: motoom@xs4all.nl
text:
On Jul 23, 2011, at 21:41, Glen Barber wrote:

> On 7/23/11 7:54 AM, Michiel Overtoom wrote:
>> =
http://bwrc.eecs.berkeley.edu/People/Grad_Students/jbeutel/wavelan_s0005-1=
1-light.htm
>=20
> The URL is too long, and it fails mdoc(7) tests with formatting the
> manual.  Looking at DragonFlyBSD's wi(4) manual, they reference the
> original page through webarchive.org, which I think is a more ideal
> solution here.
>=20
> http://leaf.dragonflybsd.org/cgi/web-man?command=3Dwi&section=3D4
>=20
> What do you think?

Yes and no... The webarchive.org link is indeed more elegant, although =
it's not clear from http://web.archive.org/web/*/http://www.wavelan.com. =
where that HCF spec can be found: it presents  a generic timeline =
instead of the 'HCF Light programming specification'. I tried to find =
the document in the captures, but couldn't find it. And even if I'd find =
it, a more specific webarchive.org URL could easily be as long (or =
longer) than that berkeley URL.=20

If the document is still relevant enough, another solution would be to =
mirror it somewhere where it can be reached with a stable and short URL.

BTW, the fact that lengths of URLs can't be longer than a certain limit =
in manpages irks me a bit, due to SEO-friendly URLs we'll probably see =
more longer URLs in the future.  OTOH, most references in manpages are =
to institutional sites instead of blog posts, and the latter show the =
tendency to become longer. No need to panic yet ;-)

Greetings,

--=20
"Learn to value yourself, which means: fight for your happiness."  - Ayn =
Rand     =20
